research_question: After an observed aggressive-flow shock in BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, or SOLUSDT USD-M perpetual futures, do causal multi-level depth consumption, replenishment, cancellation, and impact-recovery features improve calibrated post-latency executable-payoff forecasts beyond the same shallow model using top-of-book and trade-tape features only?

closed_predecessor_controls.family: static L1 and trade-tape taker direction
closed_predecessor_controls.decision: May be used only as a frozen control. More thresholds or larger models on the same static inputs are prohibited.
May be used only as a structural control. Passive fills, queue priority, and hidden liquidity may not be assumed.
Spot aggregate flow is not admitted as a directional feature without a separately frozen materially new hypothesis.

research_basis.title: The Price Impact of Order Book Events
adapted_elements: signed additions cancellations and executions rather than trade volume alone
research_basis.non_equivalence: Contemporaneous impact in U.S. equities is not a profitable forecast in crypto futures.

research_basis.title: Multi-Level Order-Flow Imbalance in a Limit Order Book
research_basis.non_equivalence: Its explanatory relationship is not evidence of post-latency executable value.

research_basis.title: Order Flows and Limit Order Book Resiliency on the Meso-Scale
research_basis.non_equivalence: NASDAQ meso-scale resiliency must be re-established for each crypto perpetual.

research_basis.title: Deep Limit Order Book Forecasting
adapted_elements: forecast metrics cannot substitute for actionability
research_basis.non_equivalence: Round 73 uses a prospective Binance event stream and a separately specified payoff gate.

research_basis.title: TLOB
research_basis.non_equivalence: A transformer is not authorized unless the fixed shallow feature test passes.

research_basis.title: When Does Order Flow Matter? State-Dependent L2 Liquidity-State Transitions in Crypto Futures
research_basis.non_equivalence: The paper uses vendor minute snapshots for a liquidity-state target; Round 73 uses official prospective 100 ms diff depth for post-latency payoff targets.

source_contract.provider: Binance official public USD-M futures interfaces
source_contract.market_calendar: continuous except observed exchange status maintenance or disconnect
source_contract.listed_product_context_rule: Any later ETF, equity, or listed-futures context requires its actual venue calendar and timestamped source; it cannot create a Binance close.
source_contract.raw_event_receive_clock: host monotonic nanoseconds plus wall-clock nanoseconds
source_contract.exchange_clock_offset: minimum-RTT estimator retained with every segment
source_contract.database_compression: DuckDB compressed structured columns with bounded transactional batches

order_book_integrity_contract.sequence_gap_policy: invalidate affected segment resnapshot and begin a new segment
order_book_integrity_contract.queue_overflow_policy: invalidate affected segment rather than drop silently
order_book_integrity_contract.reconnect_policy: bounded exponential backoff then fresh snapshot
order_book_integrity_contract.rpi_liquidity_policy: Normal depth excludes RPI liquidity. RPI or hidden liquidity is never counted as executable capacity unless separately observed and eligible for the account.
order_book_integrity_contract.liquidation_stream_limit: The force-order stream exposes at most the latest liquidation snapshot in each provider interval; absence is not zero liquidation activity.

modeling_capture_contract.reason: Official reconstructable diff-depth history is not available in the repository. Coarse snapshots or fabricated depth cannot substitute for event-level L2.
modeling_capture_contract.ongoing_collection: Append new verified segments prospectively; never relabel a short capture as multi-year evidence.

anchor_contract.shock_threshold: training-fold symbol-specific 99th percentile of absolute signed aggressive quote notional divided by trailing 60-second median quote notional

feature_contract.availability: Only events fully received before the decision watermark may be used.
frozen_control_layer: signed aggregate-trade quote flow and count
l2_state_layer: top 5 10 and 20 level notional by side
distance-weighted depth and shape slope by side
spread in ticks and basis points
impact_absorption_layer: signed additions cancellations and executions by level band
depth consumed per signed aggressive quote notional
estimated recovery half-life from strictly past shocks
funding rate next funding time and open-interest change
observed liquidation snapshot flag and notional
feature_contract.liquidation_absence_encoding: unknown since last observed snapshot, never zero activity
feature_contract.open_interest_interpolation: last observation carried forward only within 90 seconds with age included
feature_contract.funding_semantics: actual stream value and next funding time; no fixed eight-hour assumption

target_contract.entry_and_exit_price: walk the synchronized visible L2 book at the first valid state at or after each delayed timestamp
target_contract.long_payoff: sell proceeds at exit bid ladder minus buy cost at entry ask ladder fees and stress charge
target_contract.short_payoff: sell proceeds at entry bid ladder minus buyback cost at exit ask ladder fees borrow-equivalent charge and stress charge
target_contract.runtime_fee_rule: use the larger of the frozen 12 bps charge and two account-specific taker fees plus the frozen adverse charge
target_contract.primary_binary_target: strictly positive post-latency net payoff after all frozen charges
target_contract.continuous_target: post-latency net payoff in basis points
tail_targets: maximum adverse excursion in basis points
maximum favorable excursion in basis points
spread and depth stress during holding interval
target_contract.reason: A synchronized visible-book walk is a conservative quote-path counterfactual, not proof that an order would have filled.

split_contract.overlapping_shocks: removed by the frozen refractory period

staged_models: fixed logistic and Huber control on frozen L1 and tape features
fixed shallow LightGBM on the same frozen control layer
same fixed shallow LightGBM plus L2 state
same fixed shallow LightGBM plus impact absorption
model_contract.primary_comparison: impact_absorption versus L2_state using identical rows model capacity splits and targets
model_contract.closed_family_comparison: every accepted layer must also beat the frozen L1 and tape control
model_contract.compute_backend: OpenCL GPU after deterministic capability preflight; bounded CPU fallback is recorded and cannot be described as GPU
model_contract.neural_open_condition: At least two symbols must independently pass the shallow primary comparison on 30 promotion-quality days before a separately frozen TCN or TLOB challenger may be evaluated.

evaluation_contract.inference: paired day-and-integrity-segment blocked sign permutation and block bootstrap
evaluation_contract.multiple_testing: Benjamini-Hochberg false discovery rate across symbol horizon target and primary loss
evaluation_contract.unsupported_symbol_policy: disabled rather than pooled or silently substituted
evaluation_contract.accuracy_interpretation: Accuracy is mandatory but cannot pass without calibrated proper-score skill and stable after-cost value.

stopping_rule.if_capture_qualification_fails: Repair only the data contract. No model may run on incomplete or ambiguous events.
stopping_rule.if_shallow_predictive_gate_fails: Reject the impact-absorption feature family on the observed sample. Do not add a transformer AI leverage or post-hoc thresholds.
stopping_rule.if_predictive_gate_passes_but_economic_g: Retain the model only as a risk or execution-state candidate; do not claim edge or trading authority.
stopping_rule.if_both_gates_pass_on_at_least_two_symbo: Open a separately frozen 30-day prospective shadow and paired ML-only versus deterministic-veto study. No live-money authority is granted.

limitations: Prospective event-level L2 cannot truthfully provide a multi-year result until it has actually been collected for years.
Displayed L2 omits hidden and RPI liquidity and does not reveal queue position or another participant's inventory.
The provider liquidation stream is throttled and cannot measure every liquidation.
Open interest and funding are slower state variables and cannot be interpolated into subsecond facts.
A quote-path counterfactual is not an exchange fill or a profitability result.
Binance crypto perpetuals have no formal daily close. UTC days are split and resampling units only; listed ETFs and securities use their actual venue calendars.
No model architecture can repair an input layer that fails the frozen incremental and after-cost gates.
